novel_features.name: Failed-message breakdown
novel_features.description: See why messages failed last week, grouped by error code and destination country, with the dollar cost of those failures totaled.
novel_features.rationale: Requires local SQLite aggregation across synced Messages by ErrorCode and country prefix — Twilio's API returns one row per message, never an aggregation, and twilio-cli has no offline cache.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When debugging delivery, agents need the failure distribution, not 4000 individual rows. This command answers 'why is delivery degraded' in one query.

novel_features.name: Subaccount spend matrix
novel_features.description: One CSV with every subaccount's last-month spend pivoted across SMS, MMS, voice, and recording categories — the agency-billing report nobody else ships.
novel_features.rationale: Twilio's usage/records API returns one row per category per subaccount; the cross-subaccount pivot does not exist in any single API call. Steampipe can do this with a Postgres FDW; this CLI does it with one binary call.
novel_features.why_it_matters: For agency operators, this is the entire monthly billing report in one command. Agents can run it with --period thisMonth for a live forecast.

novel_features.name: Call timeline stitch
novel_features.description: Given a CallSid, return everything that happened on that call: metadata, recordings, transcriptions, conference participation — in one structured output.
novel_features.rationale: The Call → Recordings → Transcriptions → Conference chain is three separate API endpoints in every other tool. The local store collapses it to one read, and the live fallback handles uncached SIDs.
novel_features.why_it_matters: On-call engineers paged about a dropped call need the full picture in seconds, not three API round trips while the customer waits.

novel_features.name: TCPA opt-out reconciliation
novel_features.description: Find numbers that texted STOP/UNSUBSCRIBE/END/QUIT and any messages your account sent to them afterwards — the TCPA exposure check the Twilio API has no resource for.
novel_features.rationale: Twilio has no opt-out endpoint. This command performs a local temporal join over inbound and outbound Messages keyed by phone-number pair.
novel_features.why_it_matters: TCPA fines are $500–$1,500 per violation. Compliance teams audit for this quarterly; agents can spot-check it any time.

novel_features.name: Message status funnel
novel_features.description: Distribution of message terminal statuses (delivered, failed, undelivered, sent) with delivery-rate percentages and median time-to-delivery.
novel_features.rationale: Twilio's Console graphs this; no CLI or MCP exposes the numbers. Pure local groupby over the Status enum + median time delta from synced timestamps.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Operations dashboards need the funnel, not the raw rows. One query is the whole 'is delivery healthy right now' answer.

novel_features.name: Call disposition cross-tab
novel_features.description: Cross-tab of call Status (completed, busy, no-answer, failed, canceled) by AnsweredBy (human, machine_start, fax), with dollar cost per bucket.
novel_features.rationale: Voicemail-detection rates are a key Twilio Voice metric; no CLI exposes them. Pure local cross-tab on the synced Calls table.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Outbound campaign tuning depends on knowing the human-pickup rate vs voicemail rate. Agents can answer this without paging through thousands of call records.

novel_features.name: Idle number reclamation
novel_features.description: Phone numbers you are paying for but have not used to send or receive in N days — with the dollar amount you are wasting per month.
novel_features.rationale: Three-way LEFT JOIN across IncomingPhoneNumbers, Messages, and Calls — the API has no idle-number endpoint.
novel_features.why_it_matters: At $1/number/month, agencies running 100+ subaccount numbers can save hundreds annually. Agents can run it as a monthly cleanup task.

novel_features.name: Webhook orphan audit
novel_features.description: Group your IncomingPhoneNumbers by their Voice/SMS webhook URL to find single-use URLs that may be orphans pointing at deleted endpoints. Add --probe for a live HEAD check.
novel_features.rationale: Local groupby over the synced numbers table; --probe is opt-in for live verification. No Twilio tool detects orphan webhooks.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Orphan webhooks silently fail incoming calls and SMS. Quarterly audit is cheap insurance against silent dropped traffic.

novel_features.name: Number conversation feed
novel_features.description: All messages and calls involving one phone number, merged into a single timestamped timeline with in/out arrows, body, and duration.
novel_features.rationale: UNION over synced Messages and Calls where From or To matches the number. Twilio's Console has a per-number log; no CLI exposes it.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When investigating a customer complaint or fraud claim, the full back-and-forth is one query. Agents can answer 'what did we say to this number' without four searches.

novel_features.name: Error code explainer
novel_features.description: Top error codes from your recent messages and calls, with curated one-line explanations and fixes for the most common Twilio errors.
novel_features.rationale: Local groupby on synced Messages+Calls by ErrorCode joined to a curated // pp:novel-static-reference table covering the top-50 stable Twilio error codes.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Every Twilio user Googles error codes. Bundling the explanation with the count removes a constant context-switch.

novel_features.name: Sync watermark inspector
novel_features.description: Last sync timestamp and row count per resource in the local store — so you know how fresh every other analytic command is.
novel_features.rationale: Local-only watermark read from the synced store. Agents need to know freshness before they trust an aggregation.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Without freshness UX every analytic command is suspect. One read tells the agent whether to call sync first.

novel_features.name: Live failure tail
novel_features.description: Stream new messages with a status filter as they happen — twilio-pp-cli's --follow that twilio-cli does not ship.
novel_features.rationale: Polling loop calling messages:list with DateUpdated>=last_seen every N seconds; short-circuits in verify environments via cliutil.IsVerifyEnv().
novel_features.why_it_matters: Incident triage and on-call response need a live feed of failures, not a polling shell loop. One command, no daemon.

narrative.headline: Every Twilio Core feature, plus offline message and call history, FTS, and SQL-grade analytics no other Twilio tool ships.
narrative.value_prop: Twilio's official CLI is a thin Node wrapper with 1–3 second cold start and no local cache. This CLI syncs your Messages, Calls, Recordings, UsageRecords, and IncomingPhoneNumbers into a local SQLite store you can grep, SQL, and aggregate offline. It ships every v2010 endpoint as a typed Cobra command, plus 12 novel features the Twilio ecosystem does not have: delivery-failure breakdowns, subaccount spend matrices, call-trace stitches, TCPA opt-out checks, idle-number reclamation, webhook-orphan audits, and more.
narrative.auth_narrative: Twilio uses HTTP Basic auth. You can authenticate with your Account SID (AC...) plus your Auth Token, OR with a scoped API Key SID (SK...) plus its Secret. The Account SID is always required because it is part of the URL path. Run `twilio-pp-cli doctor` to verify your credentials, detect whether the key is master or scoped, and warn on parent/subaccount mismatches.

quickstart.comment: Verify auth wiring and connectivity. Reports your auth_mode (master vs scoped) and warns on AC/SK prefix mismatches before any real work.
Local-only freshness check. Tells you which resources have data and how old it is so you know whether to run sync first.
First analytic against the local store: failed messages grouped by error code and destination country with cost-of-failures total.
Find phone numbers you are paying for but have not used in 30 days. The cheapest possible monthly cleanup.
Group IncomingPhoneNumbers by webhook URL to flag orphans. Add --probe to HEAD-check each unique URL.

troubleshoots.fix: Run `twilio-pp-cli doctor`. Common causes: scoped API Key bound to a different account than the one in TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID, or a rotated Auth Token in the secondary slot.

troubleshoots.symptom: Some commands return empty for a fresh install
troubleshoots.fix: Run `twilio-pp-cli sync --full` once before the analytic commands. Then `twilio-pp-cli sync-status --json` confirms freshness per resource.
Twilio's default rate limit is ~100 req/s. Reduce parallelism with `--concurrency 4`, or open a support ticket to raise the cap.
Scoped API Keys can only operate on the account that minted them. Create a master Auth Token credential or call against the subaccount's own SK key. `twilio-pp-cli doctor` flags this.

troubleshoots.symptom: Searching Body returns nothing for known content
troubleshoots.fix: FTS reads the local store. Re-run `twilio-pp-cli sync --resources messages` to refresh. The `\\b` boundary regex in queries needs a doubled backslash on shells that interpret the single one.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for this CLI when an agent or operator needs Twilio data they cannot get in one API call: cross-subaccount aggregation, delivery-failure analysis, TCPA opt-out audits, idle-number cleanup, or any 'how was last week' question. It is also the right tool for incident triage when a CallSid needs the full Call+Recording+Transcription stitch in one command. Skip it for one-off message sends if you already have twilio-cli configured — but for any analytic or investigative workflow it is faster, offline-capable, and agent-native.

recipes.title: Weekly delivery-failure report
recipes.explanation: Runs the failure aggregation against the local store, narrows the response to the four fields that matter so an agent can summarise without parsing 4000 raw rows.

recipes.title: Last-month agency billing CSV
recipes.explanation: Walks every subaccount, calls usage/records per subaccount, pivots categories to columns, writes one wide CSV the finance team can paste straight into a spreadsheet.

recipes.title: On-call call-trace from a Slack page
recipes.explanation: Single command for the full Call → Recording → Transcription chain. Use --select with dotted paths to keep the response small enough that an agent can summarise it in a Slack reply.

recipes.title: Quarterly TCPA opt-out audit
recipes.explanation: Local temporal join finds inbound STOP/UNSUBSCRIBE messages and any subsequent outbound messages to the same number — the report compliance teams pay legal teams to construct manually today.

recipes.title: Find idle numbers wasting money
recipes.explanation: LEFT JOIN over IncomingPhoneNumbers / Messages / Calls flagging numbers with no activity in 30 days. Output is one row per number with the dollar amount being wasted per month.
